item.title: So Unhinged It Must Be Real: Elon Reportedly Orders Government to Stop Feeding the Bomb-Sniffing Dogs"Vet visits, kenneling, dog food" are "put on hold."
item.description: As Elon Musk's so-called Department of Government Efficiency continues to plunder the federal government, even the Transportation Security Administration's bomb-sniffing K9 units aren't safe. According to an email obtained by Fox News reporter Jennifer Griffin, "ALL requests for everything," for the pups, including "vet visits, kenneling, dog food, etc" have been "put on hold until we receive guidance." The email was reportedly from the TSA's Security Operations National Explosives Detection Canine Program, which trains and deploys state and local law enforcement-led canine teams. It remains unclear who authored the email or when it was sent, but given the fact that Griffin has shared what appears to be a smartphone picture of a screen, the email is possibly sensitive in nature. Futurism has reached out to the TSA for comment. Over 300 dogs complete training at the program's facilities in Texas per year, according to an official website. It costs on average $33,000 to train a canine to detect explosives. More than 1,000 TSA canine teams are deployed nationwide, including passenger and cargo screening teams. But given the latest news, the program has seemingly been ground to a halt. https://futurism.com/musk-tsa-dogs
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: Look for the helpers
item.description: https://youtu.be/lhwS06U1SnA Pres Trauma forced the cancellation of a show, in which high school students who competed for the privilege would perform with the military orchestra nicknamed "the presidents own." Because of their skin. But fuck that. And fuck anyone who would degrade these kids. So 60 Minutes, the group that was working with the military band prior and a group of military veterans who served in that band, all got together and put those kids where they belong. On stage for the world to see. This is just one of the many ways the cunts want to erode society, and chipping away at the happiness of innocent kids is an easy choice for them.
item.dc:creator: TalkingSnake

item.title: Iran Pre-1979
item.description: Additionally, this video is an exaggeration of what it was like under Shah's rule. He was only a friend to the wealthy. Final edit: There's surprisingly been an incredible amount engagement on this post. Thank you. I would also like to add that Nowruz, derived from Persian 'nōw' (new) and 'rūz' (day), is the ancient festival marking the beginning of the solar year and the arrival of spring, observed on the vernal equinox, which falls on March 20 at 09:01 UTC of this year. With roots in Zoroastrianism and pre-Islamic Persian traditions, it has been celebrated for over 3000 years across Iran, Central Asia, the Caucasus, and parts of the Middle East and South Asia. The festival embodies themes of renewal, balance, and cyclical regeneration, aligning human life with natural rhythms. Rituals such as 'Khaneh Tekani' (spring cleaning) and the 'Haft-Seen' display - featuring symbolic items representing health, prosperity, and fertility - underscore the cultural emphasis on purification and renewal. Fire-jumping ceremonies and communal gatherings further reinforce themes of transformation and continuity, positioning Nowruz as both a cultural and spiritual observance with enduring significance.
item.dc:creator: unwaver

item.title: Say Cheese - 100 Vintage Professional Pictures!
item.description:
McDonald's Girl, 1981
The Hand, 1970s
1960s hair
Grandma in Hawaii
Opera singer Fyodor Chaliapin with ballerina Iola Tornaghi, 1896
Sunglasses Hat, 1975
Family, early 1970s
1977 style - shirt has images of golf course on it!
25th Anniversary of MGM Studios with all MGM contracted stars - Garland, Gable, Hepburn, Astaire, Lassie, they are all here, 1949
Tattooed lady Betty Broadbent, Australia, 1938
Squinty
Writer Alexandre Dumas, 1859 (photo by Gustave Le Gray)
The haircut, 1930s
1958
Mrs. Maxwell at the taxidermy-laden Rocky Mountain Museum, 1875
The Sergent family on their front porch - Clover Gap Mine, Lejunior, Harlan County, KY, 1946 (photo by Russell Lee)
Early 1980s
1940
German mascot dog from World War One
The Tygart School faculty, West Virginia, 1976
1980s braids
Family, circa 1975
Children with their nanny, 1900s
Portrait of courtesans in Shanghai, circa 1875
Prom, 1960s
Charles Augustus Stanhope, 8th Earl of Harrington, 1905
The couple that perms together stays together, 1979
Emma Jean, 1940s
1982, Ohio
U.S. Capitol police officer, 1873
What in tarnation is on his collar? I'm mystified and grossed out. It can't be a....loogie can it? 1976
A boy named Pierre-Louis, 1860s
Reverend Eavens, a man with a hole in his chest, circa 1890s
Mom and daughter with the studio donkey, 1937
1960s
Sisters, 1940s
Vicent Charles, The English Bullfighter
Captain Nathaniel Flowers and wife Margaret, 1878
Oh Mom, how could you?
Two Moons and American Horse, 1870s
Glamour shot, 1980s
Cyndi Lauper and her band, Blue Angel, 1970s
Big smile, 1978
We know who is in charge!
Muttonchops, 1970s
Married couple, Japan, 1900s
Lord and Lady Teynham with their sons, 1904
1970s
Whatever the hell this is, 1980s
Burlesque dancer Precious Diamond - she also has a tail! 1960s
Early 1970s
Actress Maude Adams, 1910s
1970s
Grumpy Girl, 1860s
Prom, 1960s
Mrs. Henderson and her dog, 1916
Something spooky about this one - the eyes are unrelenting. 1972
Siblings, Canada, 1910
1920s wedding with hand tinting
Smoking and drinking with the gators
1970
The family, 1925
Japanese wedding portrait
Kentucky lady, circa 1900
Ginger family, 1975
1892, looks like a very good boy
Goofball, 1960s
1880
Before the dance, 1970s
Mae West in a film still from "Every Day's a Holiday", 1937
Cabinet card of a man dressed as Robinson Crusoe, 1888
These siblings are fantastic! 1974
Susan Porter, 1915 (photo by James Van Der Zee)
Tooth Hairstyle, 1966
Actress/singer Phyllis Le Grand by Bassano, 1911
German architect Hans Poelzig and his unfortunate hairdo, 1929 (photo by August Sander)
Love the cravat
The Divine Lady Greyhound
Thomas Ustick Walter & family, 1850s
Chaos with Grandma, 1980s
The teacher, 1870s
Little fiddler and two doves, 1898
Lace dickey and blonde over comb hair
Unknown lady, 1932 (photo by James Van Der Zee)
He looks 16, she looks 42. It can't be a married couple, can it?
Oh my
Mrs. T.M. Heffner
Family wedding photo
Big hands
1970s family
Love Mom and her mini-me, 1963
Miss Piss
The sleeveless shirt and cigarettes make this one a keeper, 1982
Hairdo, 1966
Plantation background, early 80s
Joseph & Mary Taylor, 1904
Mrs. Knights-Whittome, 1916
Beautiful mom and look at Dad's belt, early 70s
Miss Marjorie Allcock and her dog, 1911
1922
item.dc:creator: FAUST44

item.title: 9 more parasite drawings to go!
item.description:
I am currently drawing parasites for an art display that will be up for 2 months in a book store. It was only suppose to be art, but I am now making large models and having a free two day event where I’ll be showing dead and live parasites to people in a book store filled with parasite art. I’ll be making crafts and colorizing sheets that I’ll be sharing online so you guys can celebrate parasites too. Including everyone’s names and wikis down below. Timeline: Artwalk April 4th, Earth day week events 25-27th, thinking about doing a brood parasite event in May to close out the event and see if anyone wants to turn in a parasite bingo card for free parasite art. Wondering if there would be interest online as well?? Well! I got a bunch of stuff i and dying to working on! Hope you enjoy the art and you learn a little more about the world around us.
Engorged female deer tick: https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ixodes_scapularis
Non engorged female deer tick. Will be adding one more tick, the Rocky-mountain tick.
Malaria parasite which is transferred via mosquitoes. https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malaria
Bot fly larve (wiki below)
Giardia https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Giardia
Engorged mosquito. These ladies carry malaria and bot fly larvae. https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mosquito
Non human bot fly found in North America https://extension.unh.edu/resource/bot-flies-fact-sheet-0#:~:text=Our%20most%20common%20bot%20fly,roughly%205%2F8%20inch).
Non engorged mosquito
Sleeping sickness transferred via Tsetse fly https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/African_trypanosomiasis
Lamprey https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lamprey
Tsetse fly https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tsetse_fly
And a leech https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leech
item.dc:creator: LindseyRaeMunson

item.title: Baked Garlic Parmesan Potato Wedges
item.description: Baked Garlic Parmesan Potato Wedges Ingredients: 4 large russet potatoes, washed and cut into wedges 1/4 cup olive oil 3 cloves garlic, minced 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese 1 tsp paprika 1 tsp dried oregano 1/2 tsp garlic powder 1/2 tsp onion powder Salt and black pepper, to taste Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish Instructions: 1. Preheat the Oven: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper or lightly grease it. 2. Prepare the Potato Wedges: Cut the potatoes into wedges (about 8 wedges per potato) and soak them in cold water for 20-30 minutes. This helps remove excess starch for extra crispiness. Drain and pat the wedges dry with a clean kitchen towel. 3. Season the Potatoes: In a large bowl, mix olive oil, minced garlic, Parmesan cheese, paprika, oregano, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Add the potato wedges and toss until evenly coated. 4. Arrange on Baking Sheet: Place the seasoned potato wedges on the prepared baking sheet in a single layer, skin-side down. 5. Bake: Bake for 35-40 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the wedges are golden brown and crispy. 6. Serve: Sprinkle the baked potato wedges with additional Parmesan cheese and chopped parsley. Serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce (like ranch or garlic aioli). These Baked Garlic Parmesan Potato Wedges are crispy on the outside, tender on the inside, and bursting with garlicky, cheesy flavor. A perfect side dish or snack! ???
item.dc:creator: Recipesusa

item.title: Horrifying
item.description:
Source: https://bsky.app/profile/newseye.bsky.social/post/3lkl3lsuvxs2r And just in case you were gonna say "so what, they're all just gang members and I don't care about treating those humanely", no, they're not, making an already terrible thing even worse, as this post from @RenegadeSci shows: https://imgur.com/gallery/usa-government-disappearing-lgbtq-assylum-seekers-QY4jywM Here's where they're being disappeared to:
Source: https://www.npr.org/2025/03/17/g-s1-54206/el-salvador-mega-prison-cecot I have no idea if any of these men will ever see the light of day again (quite literally, because the cells have no windows). After all, a German tourist just spent 46 days in a detainment camp at the US Mexican border without actually having done anything wrong and not even the embassy or the lawyers her family hired could do anything to get her out. (https://edition.cnn.com/2025/03/04/world/german-detained-ice-intl-latam/index.html) These men? In that prison? In El Salvador? Not a chance.
item.dc:creator: ParallelParkingInABurka
